subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Zone refining is used for the purification of indium.

Zone refining is based on the principle that the impurities are more soluble in the melt than in the solid state of the metal. 

This method is very useful for producing semiconductor and other metals of very high purity, e.g., germanium, silicon, boron, gallium.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Smelting is a form of extractive metallurgy to produce a metal from its ore. Smelting uses heat and a chemical reducing agent to decompose the ore, driving off other elements as gasses or slag and leaving just the metal behind. The reducing agent is commonly a source of carbon such as coke, charcoal, and coal.

FeO is gangue and SiO2 is flux to form slag FeSiO3.

FeO+SiO2→FeSiO3

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
(a) Sodium carbonate Na2CO3 & NaHCO3 are prepared by Solvay process.

(b) Titanium is refined by Van-Arkel process.

Titanium : Van arkel method

Ti+I2⟶T1TiI4g

TiI4g→T2>T1T2TiRefined titanium+2I2g

(c) Chlorine is prepared by Deacon process.

Chlorine : Decon's process

HCl+O2⟶CuCl2H2O+Cl2

(d) Sodium hydroxide is prepared by Castner - Kellner process.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Less tendency to be soluble in melts than impurities
solution:
This process is used for refining easily fusible metals like lead and tin. The impure metal is heated on the sloped hearth of a reverberatory furnace. The metal melts and flows down leaving the impurities.

Liquation is a metallurgical method for separating metals from an ore or alloy. The material must be heated until one of the metals starts to melt and drain away from the other and can be collected. This method was largely used to remove lead containing silver from copper, but it can also be used to remove antimony minerals from ore, and refine tin.

Liquation is used to purify metals having lower melting point than impurities present in them. For example, Pb, Sn, Sb, Bi and Hg.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: The electrolytic cell which is used for extraction of aluminium is a steel vessel. The vessel is lined with carbon, which acts as cathode and graphite is used at the anode.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Calamine is the carbonate ore of zinc.It composition is ZnCO3.

Galena is also called lead glance is the natural mineral form of lead (ii) sulfide,PbS

Sphalerite is a sulfide mineral with the formula ZnS. It is the most important ore of zinc.

Siderite is a mineral composed of iron(II) carbonate (FeCO3).

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
solution:
In Electrolytic refining, the pure metal is used as cathode and impure metal is used as anode. Electrolytic refining of aluminium is called Hall-Heroult process. 

Na3AlF6 is added during electrolysis of Al2O3 to lower the melting point and increase conductivity.
Reactions at cathode and anode are as follows:
Cathode: Al3+(melt) + 3e- → Al(l)

Anode: C(s) + O2- (melt) → CO(g) + 2e-C(s) + 2O2- (melt) → CO2  (g) + 4e-

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Alumina is mixed with cryolite which acts as an electrolyte

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Calcination involves heating of carbonates or hydrated oxides in absence of air.

$$

\therefore 2 \mathrm{Cu}_{2} \mathrm{~S}+3 \mathrm{O}_{2} \stackrel{\Delta}{\longrightarrow} 2 \mathrm{Cu}_{2} \mathrm{O}+2 \mathrm{SO}_{2} ; \text { is roasting }

$$

(heating in a regular supply of air).

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: lower the melting point of alumina
solution: Fused alumina $\left(\mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3\right)$ is a bad conductor of electricity. Therefore, cryolite $\left(\mathrm{Na}_3 \mathrm{AlF}_6\right)$ and fluorspar $\left(\mathrm{CaF}_2\right)$ are added to purified alumina which not only make alumina a good conductor of electricity but also reduce the melting point of the mixture to around $1140 \mathrm{~K}$.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: is brought to molten form which is made to flow on a slope
solution:
Liquation is a metallurgical method for separating metals from an ore or alloy.

This method was largely used to remove lead containing silver from copper, but it can also be used to remove antimony minerals from ore, and refine tin.

Liquation process is used for metal having low melting point such as tin in which they are heated and brought to molten state and made to flow down the slope while impurities with higher melting point left on the top.

Liquation is similar to distillation, except instead of vaporizing the impure metal, it is melted.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Leaching is often used if the ore is soluble in some suitable solvent. The ore of tin metal is cassiterite(SnO2). Cassiterite is readily soluble in alkali. The reaction is shown below.

SnO2+2NaOH+2H2O→Na2Sn(OH)6

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Ellingham diagram is a graph showing temperature dependence of the stability of compounds. This analysis is used to evaluate the case of Reduction of metal oxides and sulphides. This diagram also used to predict the equilibrium temperature between a metal and its oxide.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: selectively prevent one component of the ore from coming to the froth.
reduce the consumption of oil for froth formation.
enhance non-wettability of the mineral particles.
solution:
In the froth floatation process, the role of the depressants is to separate two sulphide ores by selectively preventing one ore from forming froth.

For example, to separate two sulphide ores, PbS and ZnS,NaCN is utilised. NaCN forms a combination with zinc on the surface of zinc sulphide, Na2ZnCN4, preventing ZnS from foaming selectively.

4NaCN + ZnS⇒Na2ZnCN4+Na2S

options: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
A is not correct but R is correct
(3) Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is correct but R is not correct
solution:
2C(s)+O2( g)→2CO(g)

As the number of moles of gas increases entropy increases.

∆rS° is +ve

∆rGo=∆H°-T∆rS°

Slope = ∆rS° = -ve

As temperature increases ΔrG° becomes more negative thus it has lower tendency to get decomposed.

Hence, A is correct but R is not correct.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Mond process for metal refining: In this process, nickel is heated in a stream of carbon monoxide forming a volatile complex, nickel tetracarbonyl:

Ni + 4CO →330 - 350K Ni(CO)4

The carbonyl is subjected to a higher temperature so that it is decomposed giving the pure metal:

Ni(CO)4 →450 - 470K Ni + 4CO

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
During extraction of copper, iron oxide present as impurity is removed by adding SiO2 and converting it into FeSiO3

FeO+SiO2→FeSiO3          Flux          Slag

Hence,FeO is gangue and FeSiO3 is slag.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
In roasting process, metal sulphide ore are converted into metal oxide and sulphur is remove in the form of SO2 gas.

2MS+3O2⟶Δ2MO+2SO2↑

chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Impurities from the blister copper deposit as anode mud which contains antimony, selenium, tellurium, silver, gold and platinum.

options: Both the assertion and reason are correct and the reason is the correct explanation for the assertion.

solution:
Iron can be extracted from Haematite ore. The Haematite is the mineral form of iron (III) oxide, one of several iron oxides.
Haematite is not a carbonate ore. Hence, the reason is false.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
In the extraction of copper from its sulphide ore, metal is finally obtained by the oxidation of cuprous sulphide with Cu2O. ​ It is an example of auto reduction. 

Cu2S+2Cu2O →6Cu+ SO2

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: ore is heated above its melting point to expel $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ or $\mathrm{CO}_2$ or $\mathrm{SO}_2$
ore is heated below its melting point to expel volatile impurities
ore is heated above its melting point to remove $\mathrm{S}, \mathrm{As}$ and $\mathrm{Sb}$ as $\mathrm{SO}_2, \mathrm{As}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ and $\mathrm{Sb}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ respectively
ore is heated below its melting point to expel $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ or $\mathrm{CO}_2$
solution: Calcination is a process of heating a substance to a high temperature but below the melting or fusion point, causing loss of moisture, reduction or oxidation and dissociation into simpler substances.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Mond's process involves the combination of carbon monoxide with nickel readily and reversibly and gives nickel tetracarbonyl. This process is used to extract and purify nickel.

The Van Arkel technique is used to refine Zirconium or Titanium. Impurities found in Zirconium or Titanium in the form of nitrogen and oxygen are eliminated using this technique.

Copper is purified by electrolysis. Electricity is passed through solutions containing copper compounds, such as copper sulphate. The anode (positive electrode ) is made from impure copper and the cathode (negative electrode) is made from pure copper.

Mercury and zinc are purified by distillation method. Liquation is a metallurgical method to remove lead containing silver from copper and remove antimony minerals from ore.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: No Solution Available

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
The Hall–Héroult process is the major industrial process for smelting aluminium. It involves dissolving aluminium oxide (alumina) (obtained most often from bauxite, aluminium's chief ore, through the Bayer process) in molten cryolite, and electrolyzing the molten salt bath, typically in a purpose-built cell.

In this process carbon electrodes are used.

The following reactions take place 

2Al2O3+3C→4Al+3CO2

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: van Arkel method is used to purify tungsten.
The malleable iron is prepared from cast iron by oxidising impurities in a reverberatory furnace.
Cast iron is obtained by melting pig iron with scrap iron and coke using hot air blast.
Boron and Indium can be purified by zone refining method.
solution:
van Arkel method is used to purify Zirconium and Titanium. This method is very useful for removing all the oxygen and nitrogen present in the form of impurity in certain metals like Zr and Ti. The crude metal is heated in an evacuated vessel with iodine. The metal iodide being more covalent, volatilises:
Zr + 2I2 → ZrI4
The metal iodide is decomposed on a tungsten filament, electrically heated to about 1800 K.
ZrI4 → Zr + 2I2

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: pig iron, scrap iron and steel
wrought iron, pig iron and steel
solution: Cast iron is made from pig iron which is used for production of wrought iron & steel.

options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true

solution: The rotating paddle in the froth flotation process violently agitates the suspension of powdered ore in water, as well as the collectors and froth stabilisers, generating frothing.  Iron pyrites are generally avoided for extraction of iron due to environmental reasons.

chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Pb</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ag</mi></math> commonly exist in the form of sulphide ore like <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>PbS</mi></math> (galena) and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Ag</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> (Argentite) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Al</mi></math> is mainly found in the form of oxide ore whereas  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Mg</mi></math> is found in the form of halide ore.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Fluorine is present mainly as insoluble fluorides (fluorspar CaF2, cryolite Na3AlF6 and fluoroapatite 3Ca3(PO4 )2 .CaF2 ). Sea water contains chlorides, bromides and iodides of sodium, potassium, magnesium and calcium, but is mainly sodium chloride solution (2.5% by mass). The deposits of dried up seas contain these compounds, e.g., sodium chloride and carnallite, KCl.MgCl2 .6H2O.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math>  and  <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>  are produced in this process.

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math>  is mixed with  <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>  which lowers the melting point of the mixture and brings conductivity.

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> is reduced at the cathode to form <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Al</mi></math>.
solution: Hall - Heroult process for extraction of Al. Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> is electrolyte Na<sub>3</sub>AlF<sub>6</sub> reduces the fusion temperature & provides good conductivity.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Sphalerite ore: ZnS

Calamine ore: ZnCO3

Siderite ore: FeCO3

Malachite ore: CuOH2·CuCO3
It is possible to separate two sulphide ores by adjusting proportion of oil to water or by using 'depressants'. In case of an ore containing ZnS and PbS, the depressant used is NaCN.
